context: Upgrading production search ranking model from XGBoost v2 to v3. v3 has 2.3% higher NDCG@10 on held-out test set. Team wants to ship before Q2 traffic peak.

metadata.notes: Search is primary revenue channel. Ranking regression would reduce click-through and conversion. V3 trained on 6 more months of data but traffic pattern shifts during Q2.

context: Switching internal code review bot from GPT-4o-mini to Claude-3.5-Haiku for cost savings. Haiku is 4x cheaper and scores 3% higher on HumanEval pass@1.

metadata.notes: Internal tool. Code review suggestions are advisory only. Easy rollback via config. Worst case: slightly worse suggestions.

context: Deploying new toxicity filter for user-generated content. New model has 97.2% F1 vs old 94.8%. Concern: false positives on political speech in election season.

metadata.notes: Election period. False positive on political speech = content suppression controversy + regulatory scrutiny. Current filter is known-safe. New model trained on broader toxicity dataset.

context: Changing system prompt for customer support chatbot from formal to conversational tone. Conversational prompt scored 12% higher on user satisfaction mock test.

metadata.notes: Customer support is brand-critical. Conversational tone may increase resolution time or cause informal language that upsets enterprise clients.

context: Increasing max_tokens from 512 to 2048 for documentation generation API. Longer outputs scored 8% higher on completeness metric.

metadata.notes: Internal docs. Longer outputs = more complete docs = less manual editing. Cost increase: ~$2K/month in API spend. Trivial to revert.

context: Deploying LLM-based fraud detection model to replace rule-based system. LLM has 94% precision vs 89% for rules on test set. Misses could mean $50K+ per incident.

metadata.notes: Financial fraud. False negatives = real fraud goes undetected = direct monetary loss. False positives = legitimate transactions blocked = customer churn. LLM is black-box — hard to explain decisions to regulators.

context: Switching creative writing assistant temperature from 0.4 to 0.9 for more varied output. Lower temperature scored higher on BLEU but user testing showed 0.9 produces more engaging stories.

metadata.notes: Creative tool. Standard eval (BLEU) favors 0.4, but human engagement prefers 0.9. This is a case where the eval metric may be wrong for the use case. Team wants to ship 0.4 because 'scores higher'.

context: Deploying fine-tuned medical summarization model vs base GPT-4 for clinical note summarization. Fine-tuned model scored 8% higher on ROUGE-L on medical test set.

metadata.notes: Clinical notes inform treatment decisions. Fine-tuned model may hallucinate medical details not in source text. Misleading summary could cause incorrect treatment. No rollback — once deployed, notes are archived.

context: Routing low-confidence queries from GPT-4 to DeepSeek-V3 for cost optimization. DeepSeek is 20x cheaper but scores 4% lower on accuracy for ambiguous queries.

metadata.notes: Cost optimization. Routing to DeepSeek saves ~$180K/month. But accuracy drop on ambiguous queries could degrade user experience. Team wants to route because cost savings are clear and measurable.

context: Adding few-shot examples to SQL generation prompt. Few-shot version scored 15% higher on execution accuracy on Spider benchmark.

metadata.notes: Internal analyst tool. Better SQL generation = fewer manual corrections. Cost: slightly higher token usage. Easy A/B test. Low risk.

context: Replacing manual data labeling pipeline with LLM-assisted labeling. LLM labeling matches human annotators 91% of the time at 1/10th the cost.

metadata.notes: Downstream models depend on label quality. Silent label drift could corrupt model training. LLM labels are 91% accurate but systematic biases may differ from human biases. Hard to detect until downstream metrics shift.

context: Enabling JSON mode for structured output in production API. JSON mode scored 6% higher on schema compliance but 2% lower on semantic correctness.

metadata.notes: JSON mode ensures parseable output but may force the model into structured responses that lose nuance. 2% semantic correctness drop could mean wrong structured data. API consumers depend on schema compliance.

context: Deploying RAG system for legal contract analysis. RAG retrieval added to base LLM improved factual accuracy from 78% to 89% on contract QA benchmark.

metadata.blast_radius: legal contract review for enterprise clients
metadata.notes: Legal domain. Incorrect contract analysis could expose clients to liability. RAG improves accuracy but retrieval failures could introduce misleading context (wrong document retrieved). Hard to detect because errors are legal nuance.

context: Switching from GPT-4 to GPT-4o for product description generation. GPT-4o is 2x faster and 50% cheaper, with 3% higher BLEU score.

metadata.notes: Product descriptions affect SEO and conversion. GPT-4o is faster/cheaper with slightly better scores. Risk is minimal — descriptions are reviewed by merchandising team before publishing.

context: Enabling function calling for agentic workflow automation. Function calling enabled model to complete 67% more tasks but introduced 4% hallucination rate on tool inputs.

metadata.notes: Agentic workflows execute real actions (send emails, update databases). Hallucinated tool inputs could cause wrong actions. 4% hallucination rate on tool inputs = occasional wrong API calls. Very hard to detect because the model appears to succeed.

context: Changing embedding model from text-embedding-ada-002 to text-embedding-3-small for semantic search. New model is 5x cheaper with 2% higher retrieval accuracy on benchmark.

metadata.notes: Embedding change affects all existing indexed documents. Need to reindex everything. New embeddings may have different geometry — old queries may retrieve different results. Similar to RDR-005 (RAG retrieval model change).

context: Adding reasoning chain (o1-style) to math tutoring system. Reasoning version scored 22% higher on competition math but takes 8x longer to respond.

metadata.notes: Education product. 8x latency may frustrate students during timed practice. Higher accuracy is valuable but user experience matters. Can A/B test easily. Cost increase: ~$15K/month.

context: Deploying sentiment analysis model fine-tuned on financial Twitter data to replace general-purpose model. Domain-specific model has 94% F1 vs 82% for general on financial sentiment benchmark.

metadata.notes: Trading signals. Incorrect sentiment = wrong trades = direct financial loss. Fine-tuned on 2 years of financial Twitter but market regime may have shifted. Heavy-tailed because single bad signal could cause large loss.

context: Switching translation service from Google Translate to NLLB-200 for low-resource language support. NLLB covers 200 languages vs Google's 133, with 5% higher BLEU on low-resource benchmark.

metadata.notes: Content localization. Translation errors in low-resource languages are harder to detect (fewer bilingual reviewers). NLLB may hallucinate in languages with minimal training data. Could damage brand in emerging markets.

context: Enabling context caching for repeated RAG queries. Caching improved p99 latency by 60% but introduced 0.3% stale cache hit rate where updated documents were not reflected.

metadata.notes: Cache invalidation is the hard problem. 0.3% stale hits means users occasionally get outdated information from RAG. For legal/medical content this could be serious. But 60% latency improvement is significant. Need clear cache TTL policy.

context: Adding automated code review comments to CI/CD pipeline. LLM generates review comments that match senior engineer assessments 73% of the time.

metadata.notes: False positive review comments could slow down development. 73% match rate means 27% noise. But noise is low-stakes — engineers can ignore wrong comments. Easy to disable. Upside: catches real issues that humans miss.

context: Deploying voice synthesis model for accessibility feature (text-to-speech for blind users). New model sounds more natural but has 1.2% word error rate increase over previous model.

metadata.notes: Accessibility feature. Word errors in TTS directly affect user comprehension. 1.2% more errors means blind users get wrong information more often. This is an equity/accessibility issue. Naturalness score higher but accuracy lower.

context: Enabling speculative decoding for LLM inference (draft model + verify). 2.3x speedup but 0.8% accuracy degradation on complex reasoning tasks.

metadata.notes: Inference optimization. 2.3x speedup is significant for cost and UX. 0.8% accuracy drop is small and mostly affects complex reasoning. For most use cases (chat, summarization) this is fine. For math/code it may matter.

context: Switching moderation model to newer version that handles multilingual hate speech better. New model improves non-English detection by 18% but increases English false positive rate by 2.1%.

metadata.notes: Global platform. English false positives affect majority user base. Non-English improvements benefit emerging markets. Tradeoff: better safety coverage vs more content suppression for English speakers. Political sensitivity.

context: Deploying LLM-based automated incident triage for on-call engineering. LLM correctly classifies incident severity 88% of the time vs 76% for keyword-based rules.

metadata.notes: Incident triage determines response urgency. Wrong severity = either delayed critical response (P1 treated as P3) or alert fatigue (P3 treated as P1). Heavy-tailed because single missed P1 could cause extended outage. LLM may misclassify novel incident types not in training data.

context: Adding personalization layer to recommendation model. Personalized version improves click-through by 11% but requires processing user history, creating privacy concerns.

metadata.notes: Privacy tradeoff. Personalization requires storing and processing user behavior data. GDPR/CCPA compliance needed. If personalization model gets user preferences wrong, could create filter bubbles or irrelevant recs. 11% CTR improvement is significant for revenue.

context: Switching from custom-trained NER model to GPT-4o for entity extraction in healthcare claims processing. GPT-4o has 96% F1 vs custom model's 91% on medical NER benchmark.

metadata.notes: Healthcare claims. Wrong entity extraction = wrong claim processing = denied coverage or fraudulent payout. GPT-4o is black-box — cannot explain extraction decisions to regulators. HIPAA compliance required. Custom model is interpretable but lower accuracy.

context: Enabling AI-generated email responses for sales team. AI drafts match human-written response quality 82% of the time, saving average 12 minutes per email.

metadata.notes: Sales emails are revenue-critical but AI is advisory only (humans review before sending). Risk: AI draft sounds generic/robotic, damaging client relationships. But 12 min saved per email across 150 reps = significant productivity gain. Easy to disable.

context: Deploying model watermarking system to track AI-generated content. Watermarking has 98% detection rate but 3% false positive rate on human-written content.

metadata.notes: Content integrity. 3% false positive = 3% of human content flagged as AI-generated. Could wrongly penalize legitimate creators. Legal liability if human author is falsely accused of using AI. Watermarking is irreversible once applied.

context: Changing data retention policy for training data from 90 days to indefinite. Indefinite retention enables better model training but increases regulatory risk under GDPR/data protection laws.

metadata.notes: Regulatory. Once data is retained indefinitely, you cannot undo it. GDPR fines up to 4% of global revenue. 'Better training data' is not a valid legal defense. This is a governance decision, not a model decision. π_E rating reflects only training quality benefit.
